rulings.Case Title: TONY K FONG VS. HUANZHI JIANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues, Against Cross Complainant Huanzhi Jiang
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday March 14, 2016, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT TONY FONG MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues, Against Cross Complainant Huanzhi Jiang continued to April 15, 2016. Moving party to provide supplement brief not to exceed 5 pages, amended separate statement and evidence to address those facts in the separate statement that are not supported by evidence other than December 10, 2015 order (set aside on February 22, 2016 per CCP 473 motion) no later than March 25, 2016. Opposing party to provide supplemental brief, amended response to separate statement and evidence to address amended parts of the separate statement by no later than April 7, 2016. Moving party may file a reply, not to exceed 3 pages, by no later than April 13, 2016. Supplemental briefing is limited to the facts impacted by December 10, 2015 and February 22, 2016 orders. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: EMIL MICHAEL VS. JOHN DANFORTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Revised Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday March 14, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT JOHN DANFORTH Revised Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P 128.7 DENIED. The "offending" documents were filed and served on September 24, 2015 in opposition to motion to reclassify. Motion to reclassify was heard on October 7, 2015. Motion for sanctions was served on November 13, 2015 and filed on February 18, 2016. " 'The purpose of the safe harbor provisions is to permit an offending party to avoid sanctions by withdrawing the improper pleading during the safe harbor period. This permits a party to withdraw a questionable pleading without penalty, thus saving the court and the parties time and money litigating the pleadings as well as the sanctions request.' " See Malovec v. Hamrell (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 434; Banks v. Hathaway, Perrett, Webster, Pwers & Christman (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 949; Li v. Majestic Industry Hills, LLC (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 585. Defendant's motion does not serve this purpose. Also, the factual disputes regarding condition of the property before, during and after the tenancy form the basis of this case. Parties' interpretations of the facts (even considering Plaintiff's concession that the heat issue was timely resolved contrary to Plaintiff's assertion in declaration to the contrary) do not warrant terminating sanctions urged by Defendant, especially in light of Defendant's lack of due diligence in seeking sanctions for filings made on September 24, 2015. CCP 128.7(c). Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE CERON ET AL VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday March 14, 2016, line 4. DEFENDANT SALLY LIU MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Negligence cause of action does not arise out of protected activities. Causes of action under SFRO 37.10B, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and UCL violation are "mixed" causes of action and Plaintiffs have shown probability of prevailing on the merits. See Mann v. Quality Old Time (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 90 and Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169. Malicious prosecution cause of action arises out of protected activity. Plaintiffs have shown probability of prevailing on the merits. = (501/AJR)
